name: Dumbbell Fly
resistance_profile: Peak tension deep in the stretched position at the bottom of the arc; tension falls off as the dumbbells come together over the chest.
cues: Fixed soft elbow bend — hinge only at the shoulder
Feel the stretch, don't chase extra depth
Squeeze the pecs to draw the dumbbells together
execution_steps: Lie flat on a bench holding a dumbbell in each hand pressed above the chest, palms facing each other.
Set a slight, fixed bend in the elbows and keep it constant throughout the set.
Lower the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc until you feel a stretch across the chest, upper arms roughly level with the bench.
Reverse the arc and bring the dumbbells back up and together over the chest, squeezing the pecs.
Stop just short of the dumbbells touching to keep tension on the chest, then repeat.
good_when: You want a deep loaded stretch on the chest
You want chest isolation with minimal triceps involvement
Adding a stretch-biased accessory after pressing work
bad_when: You have impingement-prone shoulders at end range
You only have heavy dumbbells and can't control the stretched position
You want to move maximal load for the chest
common_errors: Bending and re-straightening the elbows, turning it into a press
Lowering too far and stressing the shoulder
Using momentum to fling the weights back up
progressions: Pause and control the stretched position each rep
